metadata.description: First-party regression fixture of severity-miscalibration cases extracted verbatim from Kim et al. 2026 (arXiv:2605.20668v1). NOT a calibrated threshold set: n is small and there is no deterministic detector for field-norm severity miscalibration (it requires domain judgment). The fixture pins the documented failure shapes so downstream prompt/protocol changes can be checked against real, traceable cases — it does not assert distributional FNR/FPR calibration.

source_paper.citation: Kim, S., Yoon, D., Gashteovski, K., Suk, J., Baek, J., et al. (2026). On the limits and opportunities of AI reviewers: Reviewing the reviews of Nature-family papers with 45 expert scientists. arXiv:2605.20668v1 [cs.CL].
source_paper.note: Author list and section anchors verified first-party against the PDF, not the KB summary (the summary's author list drifted; PDF is authoritative).

subtypes.field_norm_boundary: Severity miscalibration where the AI critique is content-correct against a discipline-neutral standard but mis-rated because it lacks the subfield's accepted-practice prior (paper W1, n=54).
subtypes.significance_boundary: Significance miscalibration from the AI meta-reviewer's habitual 'would addressing this change the core result?' formula (paper F.3.4, 56 errors).

provenance.verbatim_anchor: these are kept internally at CERN and the authors are not expected to share any data or simulation when publishing the paper

items.ai_critique: Analysis choices (simulation-fixed fit shapes, external calibration inputs, pseudoexperiment uncertainties) are not documented enough for independent reproduction; requests tabulated fit parameters and a machine-readable likelihood artifact.
items.field_norm: At CERN (LHCb), calibration and fit-configuration artifacts are maintained internally by the collaboration rather than published alongside the paper; not sharing them is the community's normal publication boundary.

provenance.verbatim_anchor: to the bacterial modeling community this paper does not read that it claims a universal law

items.ai_critique: The 'universal collapse' claim is not established beyond the studied system; requests out-of-distribution validation and tighter scope.
items.field_norm: In the bacterial modeling literature 'universal' denotes a bounded empirical statement, not an unconditional physical law; the scope-tightening request is correct but the reading of an overgeneralized universality claim reflects a missing community prior.
items.expert_verdict: harsh but correct on the methodological request; wrong on the universality reading

provenance.verbatim_anchor: I think the AI does not understand how hard ecology is! R2 of 0.36 is impressive!

items.ai_critique: Weakly predictive models (marginal pseudo-R² 0.05–0.36) are used to support strong global biodiversity projections of up to 89% species loss.
items.field_norm: By agricultural-ecology standards on variance-dominated observational cross-study data, a marginal R² of 0.36 is an unusually strong fixed-effect signal, not a weak one; the AI's reference class is from controlled experiments, not observational ecology.
items.expert_verdict: R² of 0.36 is impressive by field standards

provenance.verbatim_anchor: It's not possible to obtain intracranial data from healthy controls - it won't pass the ethical committee protocol

items.ai_critique: Requests intracranial control data that the AI treats as a feasible missing comparison.
items.field_norm: Intracranial data from healthy controls cannot pass an ethics committee protocol; the requested comparison is infeasible by the field's research-ethics norm, not merely absent.

provenance.verbatim_anchor: I don't think the ML community these days care so much about statistical rigor any more

items.ai_critique: Focuses on statistical-rigor gaps (possibly following a Nature review guideline) and rates them as substantive methodological weaknesses.
items.field_norm: The contemporary ML community weights statistical rigor lower than the Nature-family guideline implies; the critique applies a reference class the field does not currently use for this paper type.
items.expert_verdict: less concerning by current ML community norms

provenance.verbatim_anchor: adding phenotypic linkage would enrich interpretation, but it would not change the core analyses or main results

items.ai_critique: Reviewer noted the study does not incorporate phenotypic profiles (symptoms + neurocognitive disturbances) of the disorders.
items.field_norm: Connecting brain-imaging findings to clinical phenotypes is fundamental to the translational value of the work, not merely enriching; the 'does not change the core result' formula under-rates it.

provenance.verbatim_anchor: rated it Not Significant on the grounds that figure placement does not affect the paper's scientific substance

items.ai_critique: Reviewer suggested moving Figure 4 to the supplementary file.
items.field_norm: Information organization has real impact on how readers interpret results; a presentation issue is still substantive enough to be Marginally rather than Not Significant.

provenance.verbatim_anchor: rated this Significant because evaluation metrics are core methodological evidence

items.ai_critique: Reviewer noted Table 1 reports 'R-squared' for binary classification tasks (technically the wrong metric name).
items.field_norm: The underlying 5-fold cross-validation accuracy is interpretable despite the mislabelling, so the issue is presentation rather than substance; the AI over-weights presentation issues that involve technical terminology.

provenance.verbatim_anchor: in battery electrochemistry, CE values slightly over 100% are well-understood measurement artifacts

items.ai_critique: Reviewer flagged Coulombic efficiencies exceeding 100% as evidence of high reversibility lacking proper uncertainty quantification.
items.field_norm: In battery electrochemistry, CE values slightly over 100% are well-understood measurement artifacts, making this a minor concern; the AI over-weights it as a headline metric. (Also field-norm-like: the significance error is driven by a missing electrochemistry prior.)

provenance.verbatim_anchor: Both experts rated this Marginally Significant in the opposite direction in this case, but across the broader pattern

items.ai_critique: Reviewer raised concerns about safety monitoring and RF heating at unprecedented 11.7T field strength; AI reasoned it improves safety reporting but does not change the core scientific contribution.
items.ai_rating: (downgraded via 'does not change core result' formula)
items.expert_rating: Marginally Significant (experts agreed with the AI direction HERE)
items.field_norm: Cited by the paper as part of the broader under-rating pattern even though experts agreed with the AI's direction in THIS specific case. Retained as an EXCEPTION fixture: it documents that the 'does not change the core result' formula is not always wrong, so a severity check must not blanket-flag it.
items.miscalibration_direction: ambiguous (formula applied; experts concurred in this instance)
items.exception_reason: Paper explicitly notes experts rated this in the opposite (concurring) direction for this case while still citing it under the broader pattern. Not a clean positive Pattern A case; included to prevent over-flagging.
